headline: Memory Prices Rose Again, but Micron Stock Is Slumping
summary: FEATURE   Micron Technology stock was falling early Wednesday amid a wider selloff in technology stocks. However, prices for its memory chips are only heading upward.  The shares were down 3.2% in premarket trading.

headline: Meta, CoreWeave, Salesforce, Corning, Bending Spoons, and More Stocks That Explain Today’s Market
summary: FEATURE  Stocks moved mostly higher Wednesday with the big exception being chip stocks, which capped gains. Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh declined to say whether an interest-rate hike would be announced at the central bank’s meeting in July.

headline: Western Digital (WDC) Stock Gets Fair Value Boost As Analysts Reassess AI Storage Demand
summary: Western Digital’s analyst fair value estimate has been reset from $518.26 to $584.79, signaling a fresh look at what the stock might be worth in updated models. This change sits alongside active debate about how much AI driven storage demand, hard disk drive pricing trends, and execution on Western Digital’s product roadmap are already reflected in current expectations. As the article continues, you will see how these moving pieces shape the evolving narrative and how to keep track of...

headline: Micron Drops 8%, SanDisk Slumps 10%, Western Digital Falls 7% as Memory Stocks Pull Back With the NASDAQ
summary: Shares of Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) are down 8% to $1,061.44 in Wednesday morning trading, while SanDisk (NASDAQ:SNDK) stock has slid 10% to $2,051.10 and Western Digital (NASDAQ:WDC) shares have dropped 7% to $595.81. The memory group is leading a broader semiconductor pullback as the NASDAQ 100 opens the second half of the year lower. The ...
